question: Define a `struct Student` with members: an integer `id`, a `std::string name`, and a `double gpa`. Write a C++ program that declares a `Student` variable, prompts the user to enter its details, and then displays them.

test_cases.expected:
Enter student ID: Enter student name (no spaces): Enter student GPA:
--- Student Details ---
ID: 102
Name: Jane
GPA: 3.90

errors: Uses C-style I/O (`printf`, `scanf`) instead of C++ iostreams (`std::cout`, `std::cin`), which is less idiomatic C++ and can be error-prone.
Uses a fixed-size `char` array for `name` (`char name[100]`) instead of `std::string`, which is less safe (buffer overflow risk) and less flexible.

`scanf("%s", s.name)` only reads a single word for the name, truncating names with spaces. This is an efficiency issue as it requires specific user input patterns and doesn't handle common names naturally.
